message:
fix(mpsc): receiver close returns Disconnected when channel empty instead of Empty

When a receiver calls close() and drains remaining messages, the final
recv() on an empty-and-closed channel was returning TryRecvError::Empty
instead of the semantically correct TryRecvError::Disconnected. This is
because the close path only checked buffer emptiness without consulting
the receiver_dropped flag that signals intentional teardown.

Now the recv hot-path checks receiver_dropped after finding an empty
buffer and returns Disconnected, which lets callers distinguish "no
messages yet" from "channel is permanently done." Includes inline test
receiver_close_returns_disconnected_on_empty exercising the exact
sequence: send → close → recv (gets value) → recv (gets Disconnected).

Also upgrades an impossible-branch panic!() in session.rs to
unreachable!() for consistency with the project convention that
structurally impossible arms use unreachable, not panic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
